title: Generative AI Guidelines
summary: SEB is built on trust and relationships, always prioritizing the big picture, understanding client’s needs, and offering the best long-term advice. Clients value this and are willing to pay a premium for it.

columns.title: What is Generative AI?
columns.content:
#### Based on training data, Generative AI mimics human creativity

to create new content, such as text, images, music, and more.

sections.section-content: ## Content created with generative AI must follow SEB’s brand and tone guidelines to maintain consistency.

sections.title: Whenever using AI-generated content, it must:

columns.content:
1. Be reviewed by colleagues, relevant Heads, risk managers, or legal when uncertain.
2. Undergo fact-checking to ensure accuracy, especially for guides or checklists.
3. Comply with all legal and regulatory requirements.

Disclosure  
Any content generated or adjusted by AI must include a clear disclosure, as required by the EU AI Act. This applies to all formats (text, images, audio, video) and must be visible at the first interaction or exposure.

sections.title: Image Generation
sections.section-content: Any AI-generated images submitted for approval should be checked against these general requirements:

sections.title: Modern, yet timeless.Elegant, uncluttered, and effortless.Confidence. Less is more.Attention to detail. Just like in our business.

columns.text: Please also note the overall 'Instruction for Artificial Intelligence Risks' and the 'Guideline for generative AI in internal and external communication' available to download on the following links:
